metadata.name: claude-code
metadata.description: 🤖 Claude Code — delegate coding tasks to Anthropic's Claude Code CLI agent. For tech users. Use when the user wants help implementing features, refactoring code, reviewing pull requests, investigating bugs, running long coding sessions, or handing off a repo task to a coding sub-agent. Triggers on phrases like 'do this in Claude Code', 'ask Claude Code to fix it', 'have Claude review this PR', 'delegate this repo task', '用 Claude Code 改一下'. Assumes the `claude` CLI is installed and authenticated. Best for repository-scoped work where a dedicated coding agent can inspect files, propose edits, run tests, and iterate independently. Can be used for one-shot jobs or persistent coding sessions, depending on the surrounding agent framework.

metadata.name: ocr-and-documents
metadata.description: 📄 OCR and Documents — extract text and structure from PDFs, scanned documents, and common office files. For tech users. Use when the user needs to read a local or remote PDF, OCR a scan, extract text from images in documents, parse DOCX content, or prepare document text for summarization, indexing, or downstream analysis. Triggers on phrases like 'extract this PDF', 'OCR this scan', 'read this document', 'turn this file into text', '从 PDF 提取文字', '识别扫描件'. Can combine fast text extraction for born-digital PDFs with OCR pipelines for scanned documents. Typical implementations use PyMuPDF for text PDFs, OCR engines for image-heavy scans, and separate libraries for DOCX/PPTX. Best for preprocessing documents into clean text or structured markdown that other skills can use.
metadata.author: unknown

metadata.name: self-improvement
metadata.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.

metadata.name: coding-agent
metadata.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, or Pi agents via immediate background processes. Use when: (1) building or creating features/apps, (2) reviewing PRs in a temp clone/worktree, (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-line fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). All coding-agent runs start with background:true immediately. Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required. Completion notification must use openclaw message send, not system event/heartbeat.
metadata.author: unknown
